p.strong: Naive Generation: O(n³) vs KV Cache: O(n²)

ul.li: Without cache: Each new token triggers full sequence re-encoding (quadratic attention, n times → cubic)
With cache: Each new token only attends to cached history (linear per step → quadratic total)
Practical speedup: 2-5x measured (theoretical ~n/3) due to Python overhead and memory access patterns

div.p:
Each step recomputes attention over ALL previous positions Step 1: 1² = 1 ops Step 2: 2² = 4 ops
Step 3: 3² = 9 ops ... Step n: n² ops Total: O(n³)
Keys and Values cached, only new token processed Step 1: 1 op Step 2: 2 ops Step 3: 3 ops ... Step n: n ops Total: O(n²)

g.text: Σ(i=1 to n) i = n(n+1)/2 ≈ n²/2

div.p: 100-token generation: ~100x fewer FLOPs Measured: 2-5x wall-clock (Python overhead)
Memory per layer: batch × heads × seq × d_k × 4 bytes × 2 (K+V) Example (batch=1, layers=6, heads=8, d_k=64):

ul.li: 1000 tokens: 24.6 MB Linear growth with sequence length
